projects.name: channel-manager
projects.progressLabel: test expansion, On Hold batch-confirm, grid today highlight, guest sort
currentGoal: Deliver Phase 2 booking operations (Make A Reservation live, Hold Booking) and continue closing the remaining competitor parity gaps.
doneRecently: **Smoke tests expanded** — 2 new test methods added to `SeededEndpointsSmokeTests.cs`:
`GuestCrud_CreateSearchMergeDeduplicate_Work` — covers `POST /guests` (create + 409 duplicate), `GET /guests?search=`, `GET /guests/{id}` with `isVip` assertion, `GET /guests/duplicates`
`ReservationSearch_AndFolioPayment_Work` — covers `GET /reservations?search=RES`, `GET /reservations?guestId=`, folio `GET` with `folioId` assertion, `POST /folios/{id}/payments`
Suite now has **8 test methods** total; expected Passed: 8, Failed: 0 on seeded stack

**Reservations On Hold quick-filter + batch Confirm** — status filter chips (All / OnHold / Confirmed / CheckedIn / CheckedOut / Cancelled) above the list; OnHold chip shows live count badge; "Confirm All (N)" button appears when OnHold filter active — calls `PUT /reservations/{id}/status Confirmed` for each hold sequentially; `reservationQuery` now passes `status` param so the list filters server-side

**Inventory Grid today highlight** — `isSameDay` check on each date header + cell; today's header gets `bg-primary/10` + bottom border accent + primary-coloured text; today's cells get `ring-1 ring-inset ring-primary/40`

**GuestProfiles sort controls** — sortable table headers: Guest (↑↓ by last/first name), Stays (↑↓ by bookingCount), Total Spent (↑↓ by totalSpent); client-side sort on fetched list; clicking the same header toggles direction; default sort: name A→Z
**FrontOffice board date picker** — already implemented (date `useState` + `<input type="date">` wired to `today-board/{id}?date=`); verified present and functional

**Inventory Grid keyboard navigation**: `useEffect` global `keydown` listener — `ArrowLeft` calls `goBack`, `ArrowRight` calls `goForward`; guard skips when a cell is being edited or focus is on an input. `goBack/goForward/goToday` wrapped in `useCallback` for stable references. Hint text updated to mention `← →`.

**CSV export date range filter**: Export now calls the API directly with `from`, `to`, `status`, `search` params (up to 500 rows). Filename includes the date range. Button shows "Exporting…" while fetching. Uses `csvExporting` state instead of the existing `reservationQuery` data.

**Batch guest merge**: `handleMergeAll(targetId)` loops all non-target duplicate profiles sequentially, calling `POST /guests/{sourceId}/merge-into/{targetId}` for each. Shows per-step progress text ("Merging 1 of 3…"). `mergeError` and `merging` states replace the old `useMutation`. Backdrop click is disabled while merging. Modal close resets error state.

**isVip fix**: Added `isVip = guest.IsVip` to both list and detail projections in `GuestsController`. Added `isVip: boolean` to `GuestDto` interface in `api.ts`. Removed `as any` casts from `GuestDetail.tsx` and `GuestProfiles.tsx`.

**Inventory Grid date navigation**: Changed `from` from fixed `startOfToday()` to navigable state. Added `WINDOW = 14` (2-week view). Prev/Next/Today buttons in a grouped border control. `dateHeaders` now generates 14 items instead of 90 — grid is much more usable.

**Reservations CSV export**: "Export CSV" button in Reservations header — generates client-side CSV (Ref, Status, Check-In, Check-Out, Total, Channel) from the already-fetched list and triggers a download. No new backend endpoint needed.

**Guest deduplication**: `GET /api/v1/guests/duplicates` returns groups of profiles sharing an email with booking counts. `POST /api/v1/guests/{sourceId}/merge-into/{targetId}` re-points all reservations from source → target, merges notes + VIP flag, deletes source. `GuestProfiles.tsx` shows an amber warning banner listing duplicate email groups; clicking a group opens a merge modal where staff picks the profile to keep.

**Record Payment on Folio**: `POST /api/v1/folios/{folioId}/payments` added to `FolioController` — validates amount, parses `PaymentMethod` by name, adds `FolioPayment`, updates `TotalPayments` + `Balance`. `FolioPage.tsx` has a green-tinted `RecordPaymentForm` (method selector, amount, optional reference) toggled by "Record Payment" button; Post Charge and Record Payment forms are mutually exclusive.

**Reservations search bar**: `ReservationFilterRequest` extended with `Search?` param; `ReservationsService.ListAsync` applies `ILike` filter on `InternalRef` and `ExternalBookingRef`; controller accepts `?search=`. `Reservations.tsx` has a 350ms debounced search input above the list tab with match count feedback.

**MakeReservation guest pre-fill**: Reads `?guestId=` from URL. Sets `existingGuestId` on the guest state; Step 1 advances directly to Step 3 (skipping Step 2) when pre-filled. Pre-filled guest banner shows name + email on the Review step. GuestDetail "New Reservation" button now passes `?guestId=${id}`.

**Availability Grid per-cell stop-sell**: `cellStopSellMutation` calls `POST /inventory/stop-sell` or `open-sell` with single date + roomTypeId. Right-clicking any cell opens a context menu offering "Stop Sell" or "Open Sell" depending on current state. Stop-sell cells now show ⊘ instead of —. Hint text added to subtitle.

**CI acceptance test job**: Added `acceptance` job to `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — spins up Postgres + API with bootstrap seed, runs `dotnet test ChannelManager.AcceptanceTests`, uploads results and API log as artifacts. Docker job now requires `acceptance` to pass.

**Guest Profiles — new-guest creation**: Added `POST /api/v1/guests` to `GuestsController` — validates required fields, deduplicates by email (409 on conflict), creates `GuestProfile`. `GuestProfiles.tsx` now has a debounced search bar (350ms, with clear button) and an inline `NewGuestPanel` form that opens on "+ New Guest" click, posts to the new endpoint, and refreshes the list on success.

**Folio page — charge grouping + inline post-charge**: `FolioController.GetReservationFolio` response now includes `folioId`. `FolioDto` type updated. `FolioPage.tsx` rebuilt: balance summary chips (Subtotal / Tax / Total / Balance Due with colour), charges grouped by department (Room/F&B/Service/Parking/Other) with coloured badges + per-group subtotals, payments section with green amounts, inline `PostChargeForm` (department dropdown, description, quantity, unit price, live total preview, posts to `POST /folios/{folioId}/charges`).

**Channels push-rates**: `POST /api/v1/channels/{connectionId}/push-rates` — resolves room types + active rate plans, calls `PushRateUpdateAsync` for each combination, updates `LastPushAt`, returns counts and date range. `ChannelsController` now injects `RatesDbContext`. Channel cards now show both "Push Inventory" and "Push Rates" buttons with individual spinners and success/error feedback.

**guestId server-side filter**: Added `?guestId=` param to `GET /api/v1/reservations`. Updated `ReservationFilterRequest`, `ReservationsService.ListAsync`, `ReservationsController.List`, and `GuestDetail.tsx` — stay history now fetches server-side instead of loading all property reservations client-side.

**Analytics range backfill**: Added `POST /api/v1/properties/{id}/analytics/build-snapshot-range?from=&to=` (max 90 days, skips errors per day). Added `BuildHistoryPanel` dashed-border card at top of Analytics page with date pickers and "Build Range" button; invalidates all analytics queries on success.

**Channels push-update**: `ChannelsController` extended with `POST /api/v1/channels/{connectionId}/push` — resolves active room types from inventory if none mapped, pushes next 7 days' inventory to each via `IChannelSyncJobHandler`, updates `LastPushAt`. `Channels.tsx` rebuilt: KPI strip, error alert banner, `ChannelCard` with "Push Update" button (shows success/error inline), last-sync relative timestamp, Wifi/WifiOff icon per status.

**BDD acceptance tests**: `MakeAReservation.feature` added with 3 scenarios (make reservation → Confirmed, availability → rate plans, hold booking → OnHold). `BookingOperationsParity.feature` updated from Draft/old-endpoint to OnHold/`/reservations/hold` and `/reservations/make`. `AcceptanceBindings.cs` updated with bindings for `I create a hold booking`, `I make a reservation`, `I request availability`, `the availability response contains at least {n} rate plans`.

**Analytics page** (replaced static placeholder): Revenue tab (summary chips + stacked bar chart by room/F&B/other), Occupancy tab (dual-axis line: occupancy % + RevPAR), Channels tab (horizontal bar chart + breakdown table). All tabs have date-range pickers with 7d/30d/90d/YTD presets. Wired to live `/analytics/revenue`, `/analytics/occupancy`, `/analytics/channels`. Shows empty-state with hint to build snapshots from Dashboard.

**Night Audit** (replaced placeholder): Two-step confirmation (amber warning card → run button) before executing `POST /api/v1/folios/night-audit/{propertyId}?date=`. Shows success card with business date on completion. Includes best-practice tips. Fixed authorization from `NightAuditor,SuperAdmin` to `[Authorize]` so any authenticated staff can run. Added `GET night-audit/{propertyId}/status` endpoint. Response changed from `204 NoContent` to `200 OK` with `{ ran, businessDate }`.

**Guest Detail** (enriched): Avatar + name header with VIP badge, KPI strip (total stays with completed count, total spent with upcoming count, last stay date), stay history clickable list (filtered to current property, showing status badge + dates + amount), profile details card (DOB, notes). "New Reservation" quick-action button.

**PackageManager full update**: Frontend `updateMutation` now sends all fields (name, description, code, type as int, minNights, priceModifier, priceModifierType, validFrom, validTo, imageUrl) so inline edit saves the complete package, not just status+price.

**ReservationDetail** — full status lifecycle actions (Confirm, Check In, Check Out, Cancel) with correct per-status button display; OnHold amber banner; status badge colour map for all 7 statuses; Folio link
**Reservations list** — OnHold and Draft status filter options added; `getStatusColor` updated to cover all 7 statuses with amber for OnHold

**Dashboard KPI** — added `POST /api/v1/properties/{id}/analytics/build-snapshot` to `AnalyticsController`; dashboard now has a "Refresh KPIs" button that triggers the build then invalidates the KPI query

**PackageManager** — replaced placeholder with fully live page: status filter, list view with type/status/price badges, inline create form (name, code, type, status, price modifier, validity range), inline edit form wired to `PUT /packages/{id}`
**Audit pass** — identified and fixed three issues:
`ReservationStatus` enum was missing `OnHold` value — added it before `Confirmed`

`HousekeepingController` only exposed `room-chart`; the service had `CreateTaskAsync`, `UpdateTaskAsync`, `GetTasksByPropertyAsync` fully implemented but not wired — added `GET/POST tasks` and `PUT tasks/{id}/status` endpoints
Settings page address fields appeared editable but weren't saved — replaced with read-only display cards with a clear "contact support" note
**Hold Booking** (`POST /api/v1/reservations/hold`):
Accepts propertyId, roomTypeId, checkIn/out, adults, guestName (minimum), optional email/phone, optional ratePlanId, holdUntil datetime, notes
Creates an `OnHold` reservation; re-uses existing guest by email if found
Frontend: `HoldBooking.tsx` — single-page form with 3 cards (booking details, guest, hold settings); amber-coloured CTA; navigates to reservation on success
Wired `Hold Booking` sidebar entry and `/reservations/hold` route
**Housekeeping — live board** (replaced placeholder entirely):
Room Board tab: same colour-coded grid as Room Chart but pulls from the housekeeping `room-chart` endpoint (includes in-progress task counts as orange badges on room cells); auto-refreshes every 60s

Tasks tab: filterable list (All / Pending / InProgress / Completed) with badge counts, Start/Done/Hold quick-action buttons, inline Create Task form (room selector from live room chart data, task type, description)
Both tabs share the same `hk-room-chart` query cache so task mutations update the board immediately
**Smoke tests** — added `Phase1Phase2_BookingOperationsAndRoomEndpoints_Work`:
Covers: room types list, rooms list, availability endpoint, Make A Reservation (→ Confirmed), Hold Booking (→ OnHold), housekeeping tasks list, property settings GET, KPI now returns 200
Suite now has 6 test methods; expected result: Passed: 6, Failed: 0
Expanded bootstrap room seeding:
Added second room type: **Standard Twin** (`b1b1...b1b1`) with 10 rooms, mini-bar amenities
Added 6 more deterministic rooms across both types (rooms 102–203) with varied housekeeping statuses: VacantClean, VacantDirty, OccupiedClean, OccupiedDirty, OutOfOrder (with OOO reason), VacantDirty
Room Chart now shows 7 rooms across 2 room types on a fresh seeded demo stack
Updated `docs/TEST_IDS.md` with all 7 new IDs and expected Room Chart / room-types smoke endpoints
Added **Make A Reservation** backend (`src/api/ChannelManager.Api/Controllers/MakeReservationController.cs`):

`POST /api/v1/reservations/make` — accepts propertyId, roomTypeId, ratePlanId, checkIn/out, adults, children, either `existingGuestId` or inline guest (first/last/email/phone). Auto-reuses existing guest profile by email. Looks up rate plan base price (falls back to date-specific rate record if available). Creates **Confirmed** reservation. Returns full booking summary.

`GET /api/v1/reservations/availability?propertyId=&checkIn=&checkOut=&adults=` — returns all active rate plans with computed nightly rate and total for the date range (used to populate Step 1 of the wizard)
Built **Make A Reservation** 3-step wizard frontend (`src/web/src/pages/reservations/MakeReservation.tsx`):
Step 1 — Dates & Room: date pickers, adult/child count, room type selector (card grid), rate plan selector (shows nightly + total price)
Step 2 — Guest: toggle between "Find Existing Guest" (live search by name/email, shows stay history) and "New Guest" (inline form with email de-dupe)
Step 3 — Review: full booking summary card, total price highlight, confirm button
On success navigates directly to the new reservation detail page
Wired `Make Reservation` nav entry in sidebar (FRONT OFFICE section) and `+ New Reservation` button on Reservations list page
Route `/reservations/make` registered before `/reservations/:id` so static segment wins
Fixed dashboard KPI 404:
`AnalyticsService.GetKpiSummaryAsync` now returns a zero-filled in-memory `DailySnapshot` instead of throwing `NotFoundException` when no snapshot has been built for today
Dashboard now renders cleanly on fresh seeded environments without browser console errors
Cleaned up duplicate sidebar navigation:
Removed standalone "Booking Report" and "Credit Report" nav items — both are accessible as tabs inside Reservations
Added ROOMS section to sidebar with "Room Chart" and "Room & Rate Plan" entries
Added backend room management endpoints in `PropertiesController` (Inventory module):
`GET /api/v1/properties/{propertyId}/rooms` — rooms grouped by room type with housekeeping status
`GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/v1/properties/{propertyId}/room-types` — room type CRUD
`PUT /api/v1/properties/{propertyId}/settings` — property settings update (name, timezone, currency, star rating, amenities, photos, policies)
Added rate plan CRUD to `RatesController`:
`POST /api/v1/properties/{propertyId}/rates/rate-plans`
`PUT /api/v1/properties/{propertyId}/rates/rate-plans/{ratePlanId}`
`DELETE /api/v1/properties/{propertyId}/rates/rate-plans/{ratePlanId}`
Built **Room Chart** page (`src/web/src/pages/rooms/RoomChart.tsx`):
colour-coded grid of all rooms grouped by room type
housekeeping status legend with live counts (VacantClean, VacantDirty, OccupiedClean, OccupiedDirty, OutOfOrder, OutOfService)
OOO flag and tooltip on affected rooms
Built **Room & Rate Plan admin** page (`src/web/src/pages/rooms/RoomAdmin.tsx`):
Room Types tab: list, add, edit, soft-delete room types with amenity tag editor and occupancy/count fields
Rate Plans tab: list, add, edit, soft-delete rate plans with type selector and base price field
Inline form editing with save/discard flow
Replaced placeholder **Settings** page with full hotel-admin tabs (`src/web/src/pages/settings/Settings.tsx`):
General Details: property name, star rating, timezone, currency, address — live PUT to `/settings`
Amenities: tag editor wired to `/settings`
Photo Gallery: URL list with image preview and delete, wired to `/settings`
Property Policy: check-in/out times, cancellation window/fee, min/max stay — wired to `/settings`
System Settings: read-only runtime display (timezone, currency, business date, status)
My Profile: read-only account info from auth store
Wired Room Chart (`/properties/:id/room-chart`) and Room Admin (`/properties/:id/room-admin`) routes in router
TypeScript type-check passes clean across all new/modified files
Documented the canonical spec location and delivery standard:
`docs/SPEC.md` is now explicitly the source of truth for product behavior
added BDD-first acceptance expectations and TDD red-green-refactor guidance
aligned the spec's platform version claims with the current codebase target (`.NET 8` / `EF Core 8`)
updated `README.md` so contributors can find the spec, status, and work log quickly

Confirmed the repo contains substantial breadth: 12 API controllers, 20+ application/service files, tenant-aware DbContexts, OTA adapters, payments, analytics, direct booking, and a React/Vite frontend with routed pages.
Confirmed the frontend builds successfully with `npm run build`.
Confirmed the current refactor heavily simplifies API wiring and OTA adapter implementations rather than adding net-new surface area.
Re-validated that `README.md` contains a warning not to trust “fully complete” claims at face value.
Added a first backend auth slice:
`POST /api/v1/auth/login`
`POST /api/v1/auth/refresh`
shared auth DTOs
Made JWT handling internally consistent by issuing JWT refresh tokens with a `token_type` claim instead of generating opaque refresh strings that the existing validation path could not understand.
Updated the web client to persist auth state and automatically send `X-Tenant-Slug` when the authenticated user includes one.
Updated the tenant middleware/auth pipeline ordering so authenticated requests can recover tenant slug from JWT claims when the header/subdomain is missing.
Added a session-level implementation log in `docs/WORK_LOG.md` for future agents.
Retargeted the backend solution from `net10.0` to `net8.0` so it builds with the available Homebrew SDK.
Added repo-local NuGet config (`NuGet.Config` and `src/api/NuGet.Config`) so restore succeeds even when the machine-level `nuget.org` source is disabled.
Verified the backend locally:
`dotnet restore src/api/ChannelManager.sln` succeeds
`dotnet build src/api/ChannelManager.sln --no-restore` succeeds
the API host starts and returns `200` on `/health/live` and `/health/ready`
Added a `NoOpEmailProvider` so messaging and tenant-admin no longer depend on a missing DI registration just to run.
Updated dashboard/reservations frontend pages to target the property-scoped backend endpoints that currently exist.
Added an authenticated `GET /api/v1/properties` endpoint (Inventory module) to support frontend property bootstrap.
Wired frontend property bootstrap in `AppLayout`:
fetches properties at authenticated app startup
hydrates property store and prefers `user.propertyId` when available
keeps URL `:propertyId` and selected property in sync
Updated the property store to preserve selected property across property-list refreshes.
Fixed channel sync update semantics in `ChannelSyncService`:
removed `AsNoTracking()` from push connection queries so `LastPushAt` writes persist
removed unsafe parallel fanout over a shared EF `DbContext` in push flows
Fixed maintenance frontend contract drift by switching to `GET /api/v1/properties/{propertyId}/maintenance` and adapting backend maintenance DTO/enums to the existing work-order UI shape.
Added missing backend endpoints used by existing frontend pages:
`GET /api/v1/channels` for the channels page
`GET /api/v1/guests` and `GET /api/v1/guests/{id}` for guest list/detail pages
Added `GET /api/v1/reservations/{reservationId}/folio` compatibility endpoint so the existing folio page route can resolve against current backend data.
Moved `GET /api/v1/channels` into API host assembly after runtime discovery checks, and confirmed the endpoint now resolves through auth middleware (`401` unauthenticated instead of `404`).
Performed live unauthenticated route smoke under `dotnet run`:
`/health/live` -> `200`
`/api/v1/properties`, `/api/v1/channels`, `/api/v1/guests`, `/api/v1/reservations/{id}/folio` -> `401`
Started backend hardening implementation:
introduced SMTP-capable email provider and configurable no-op fallback behavior in core DI
tightened tenant-admin service validation paths (plan parsing, email normalization/validation, inviter validation, duplicate invitation prevention, invite-accept password/name checks)
switched tenant-admin invitation password hashing to shared core `PasswordHasher`
constrained tenant-admin audit-log retrieval to tenant-id markers instead of returning broad unscoped logs
Re-verified backend build/start after hardening changes.
Added engineering handoff artifact: `docs/SYSTEM_DESIGN_OVERVIEW.html` (architecture, tech stack, glossary, and key flow concepts).
Added optional startup bootstrap path (config-gated) to initialize missing public/tenant tables and seed local login context for validation runs.

`/api/v1/guests` -> `200` (empty items)
`/api/v1/reservations/{id}/folio` -> `404` for unknown reservation (expected)
`/api/v1/admin/tenants` -> `200`
Fixed reservation API runtime failures (`500`) caused by Npgsql v8 JSON handling by enabling dynamic JSON through a shared `NpgsqlDataSource` wired into all registered DbContexts.
Expanded bootstrap deterministic sample IDs/data beyond guest/reservation/folio:
seeded chain/property/room type/room/channel connection/sync log IDs

`/api/v1/reservations/{id}/folio` -> `200`
Added deterministic handoff docs at `docs/TEST_IDS.md` for cross-agent validation consistency.
Hardened bootstrap table initialization behavior:
added mapped-table existence pre-check to skip unnecessary `CreateTablesAsync` calls for fully initialized contexts
kept tolerant catch/continue for existing table/schema states to avoid bootstrap crash in overlapping-module table-name scenarios (for example `Requests`)
Added automated backend smoke test suite:
new project `src/api/ChannelManager.SmokeTests`
validates auth + deterministic seeded endpoints against a running API via env-configured base URL and credentials
added to solution and verified passing locally

`GET /api/v1/reservations/reports/booking?propertyId=...&from=...&to=...&status=...`

API served on `http://127.0.0.1:5000`

Hardened tenant-admin runtime behavior so it no longer returns fabricated empty membership data:
tenant list `userCount` now derives from persisted billing-contact and accepted invitation signals

Re-checked feature parity against the live Asiatech admin on May 12, 2026 in read-only mode:
logged into `https://www.asiatech.in/booking_engine/admin`
confirmed local parity is now solid for:
dashboard shell
reservations list
booking report
credit report
today check-in/out board
linked-rates / one-click-rate / seasons / OTA discounts / linked-rules rate tooling
confirmed the biggest remaining parity gaps are:
room chart + room/rate-plan admin
hotel-details/system-settings breadth
hold-booking + make-reservation workflows
real user-management/reporting/billing-extension surfaces
updated `docs/competitive/ASIATECH_COMPARISON_PLAN.md` to match the current codebase instead of the older pre-Sprint-A/B comparison
inProgress: The worktree is still actively dirty across:
API host wiring (`Program.cs`)
OTA adapters (`AgodaAdapter`, `AirbnbAdapter`, etc.)
payments (`StripePaymentGateway`)
tenant admin services/persistence
many project files retargeted to `net8.0`
Current diff stats are still dominated by deletions, indicating an unfinished simplification/refactor rather than a settled implementation pass.
The repo now has a working local backend build/start path and property bootstrap wiring, but runtime correctness beyond targeted smoke/build checks still needs module-by-module validation.
nextUp: **CI green run** — trigger a push to `codex/runtime-stabilization-pass` and confirm all CI jobs pass: backend unit, acceptance (Reqnroll), browser-e2e (Playwright).
**Reservations pagination** — list fetches up to 100 rows; add Load More / pagination controls for properties with large reservation volume.

**Housekeeping assignment** — `HousekeepingTask.AssignedToUserId` is stored but not exposed in the create form; add an assignee select that lists team members (from tenant-admin users) so tasks can be delegated.
**Guest VIP toggle** — `isVip` is returned and displayed but staff can't set it from the UI; add a toggle on GuestDetail that calls `PATCH /guests/{id}` with `{ isVip: true/false }`.
**Channels sync logs** — show the 10 most recent sync log entries per channel on the channel card (status, timestamp, error message) so staff can diagnose push failures without checking server logs.
Add missing operator/admin reports:
revenue
occupancy
inventory logs
rate logs
administration
payment
Add missing extensions/billing surfaces:
request money
rate widget
WhatsApp transactions
services/renewals/help-center UI
Decide whether to add a first-class public tenant-user membership relation, or keep the current billing-contact + invitation-derived tenant-admin model for this phase.
Remove the dashboard analytics KPI `404` so the demo no longer emits browser console errors on first load.
Clean up duplicate sidebar navigation entries so the manual demo flow is less confusing.
Reconcile remaining frontend/backend contract mismatches on pages that still assume richer DTO shapes than current API responses.
Decide whether to harden the placeholder `NoOpEmailProvider` path or replace it with a real configured provider implementation.
Validate first green GitHub Actions run and tune any CI-only flake/timeouts if surfaced.
Decide whether to extend the demo server to support SignalR websocket proxying, or keep the current demo path focused on core dashboard/report/rates flows.
blockers: Automated tests now exist at two levels:
backend API smoke/E2E (`src/api/ChannelManager.SmokeTests`)
browser E2E (`src/web/e2e`)
Browser E2E in CI depends on startup timing for API/web services; if CI runners are slow, timeout tuning may be required.
The local demo web server currently proxies HTTP traffic to the API and is suitable for primary workflows, but it does not yet implement websocket upgrade handling for full SignalR parity.
The backend currently depends on the repo-local NuGet config to recover from a machine-level disabled `nuget.org` source; future environments may still need the same fix.
BDD/TDD expectations are now documented, but the repo does not yet have a dedicated feature-spec folder or selected BDD runner enforcing that workflow.
The new properties bootstrap endpoint currently maps property contact fields (`phone`, `email`, `website`) to empty placeholders because those fields are not present on the current property entity.
`NoOpEmailProvider` keeps runtime DI healthy but does not actually deliver mail, so invitations/messages are still operationally stubbed.
Tenant-admin is more truthful than before, but it still depends on heuristic membership reconstruction because the core public schema lacks a dedicated tenant-user relation.
The dashboard still issues a `GET /api/v1/properties/{propertyId}/analytics/kpi` request that returns `404`, which shows up as browser console errors during manual demo use.
The sidebar currently shows duplicate navigation entries for several sections, which is confusing in manual walkthroughs even though the linked flows still work.
Several user-facing pages needed for competitor parity are still placeholder-level rather than operational:
`src/web/src/pages/settings/Settings.tsx`
`src/web/src/pages/housekeeping/Housekeeping.tsx`
`src/web/src/pages/packages/PackageManager.tsx`
The docs still overstate production readiness relative to the current codebase state.
The auth slice currently uses stateless JWT refresh tokens; revocation/rotation persistence is not yet implemented.
Tenant-aware login/runtime behavior is improved, but tenant context still depends on a resolvable tenant slug. Local multi-tenant developer ergonomics may need a more explicit tenant-selection path.
`.env` exists locally, so environment-sensitive work should avoid exposing secrets in commits or notes.
repoNotes: Backend appears architecturally broad but not yet proven end-to-end:
multi-tenant context and schema-aware DbContexts exist
core PMS modules (inventory, reservations, front office, folio, maintenance, guest experience, messaging, analytics, direct booking) all have service/controller code
several frontend pages are still static or only lightly wired, so web breadth exceeds current runtime integration depth
Highest-confidence integration gaps identified in this session:
several UI screens still assume richer domain data (for example full property contact data) than the current entities expose

tenant-admin now derives membership from billing-contact and invitation records, but it still lacks true tenant-to-user relational modeling in core entities, so some admin views remain heuristic/limited even after validation hardening

narrative.currentGoal: Deliver Phase 2 booking operations (Make A Reservation live, Hold Booking) and continue closing the remaining competitor parity gaps.
narrative.inProgress: The worktree is still actively dirty across:

projects.name: pdf-esigner
projects.progressLabel: Release-facing app/docs/security work is merged; Aadhaar work is hardened but gated off, India Desk is disabled, and remaining tasks are deployment, launch operations, and monitored rollout
currentGoal: Push the hardened release batch, keep the public app/docs truthful about what is live, and deploy only the stable customer-facing surface while leaving incomplete Aadhaar/India-specific flows disabled by default.
doneRecently: Hardened the Aadhaar eSign callback flow before allowing it anywhere near production:
added callback-secret validation for Digio webhook handling
added the missing `aadhaar-esign-status` edge function
changed completion handling so a failed completion pipeline does not still mark a session as completed
added schema support for Digio document/reference identifiers and eSign metadata in `supabase/migrations/20260504_aadhaar_esign.sql`
Gated Aadhaar eSign behind `VITE_AADHAAR_ESIGN_ENABLED=false` so the feature stays off unless the full Digio secret/webhook setup is intentionally enabled in an environment.
Disabled `India Desk` from the live app surface and redirected `/india-desk` to `/send` so partially finished regional flows are not user-reachable.
Updated repo ignore rules so Flutter-generated machine-local caches and ad hoc artifacts do not drift back into commits.
Finished another public SEO pass and deployed it live:
added standard favicon/search assets plus `site.webmanifest`
removed `/login` from the sitemap and added explicit public route coverage for `/docs` and `/tools`
added more keyword landing pages for comparison/workflow intent (`/hellosign-api-alternative`, `/online-pdf-signer`)
added additional keyword pages for exact SDK/API intent (`/esignature-sdk`, `/pdf-signing-api`)
strengthened blog/docs internal linking and structured data for crawlability
rewrote blog metadata toward higher-intent search phrasing and fixed broken markdown links that pointed at nonexistent `/guides/*` and `/api/*` routes
added a clearer “choose your integration path” conversion section on `/developers`
fixed docs-page SEO dispatch so docs canonicals/titles no longer rely on a broken fallback variable

Prepared an external SEO/distribution launch kit with Product Hunt copy, listing blurbs, Search Console follow-through URLs, and community-post drafts in `docs/seo-external/DISTRIBUTION_LAUNCH_KIT.md`.
Added a condensed execution guide for launch day in `docs/seo-external/LAUNCH_DAY_CHECKLIST.md` so Product Hunt + Search Console + first-wave distribution can happen in one focused session.
Added dedicated launch-ready copy files for Product Hunt, software directories, and first-wave social/community posts under `docs/seo-external/`.
Refreshed the Flutter smoke app so mobile validation better reflects the real product direction:
Android package/application identity moved from the example placeholder to `com.aoexl.mobile`
iOS scaffolding was added to the smoke app
the app now has a hosted-product shell plus a native SDK demo entry point instead of only a single test screen
Flutter bridge packaging was kept aligned with the current SDK integration surface
Merged the SDK/viewer stabilization and outreach split into `master`:
`@aoexl/sign` is prepared as `1.2.7`
SDK consumers can use `base` for self-hosted assets
first-load zoom can use `config.initialViewMode: "fit-width"`
flattened-document messaging is configurable
small PDFs eager-render to reduce short-document scroll stutter
engine asset loading starts in parallel with license validation
stale signing fields are cleared when opening/uploading another PDF
SDK tests pass with `57 passed`
Removed outreach from the customer-facing app route/sidebar and moved it into the standalone private `outreach-app` workspace.
Added internal change documentation at `docs-site/internal/operations/change-summary-2026-04-20.md`.
Added `Images to PDF` to the public PDF tools surface, including single/multi-image conversion, reorder, page size, fit mode, margins, and output naming.
Reworked the public landing/navigation so PDF tools are the lead wedge and the marketing nav exposes the real current feature surface more clearly.
Replaced stale third-party-branded public demo screenshots and sample PDFs with neutral Aoexl demo collateral.
Cleaned the public demo viewer chrome so the embedded workspace no longer collides with the marketing nav while scrolling.
Added a compact mobile marketing menu so the public header exposes product, tools, developer, resource, pricing, sign-in, and contact destinations on small screens.
Reworked `/developers` into a more documentation-like in-app hub that links quickstarts, guides, API docs, webhooks, and dashboard operations while preserving existing SDK/demo/webhook functionality.
Added stable `/developers#...` deep links for the Developer Hub docs/articles and major page sections.
Added multi-tenant MCP distribution groundwork:
in-app MCP API key generation/revocation in Settings
per-user API-key validation flow for the hosted MCP Worker
docs-site guide for Claude Desktop / Cursor MCP setup
publishable `aoexl-mcp` npm wrapper plus Smithery / MCP Registry metadata
public connector repo now lives at `rails2track/aoexl-mcp`
Added app/docs SEO basics:
route-aware metadata for public app routes
`noindex,nofollow` for signer/login/authenticated routes
`robots.txt` and `sitemap.xml` for both `aoexl.com` and `docs.aoexl.com`
Stabilized the hosted signer flow across desktop/mobile, including compact signer chrome, floating progress UI, drag/touch improvements, next-field navigation, and completion handling.
Fixed live signing request creation, reopening/cancel semantics, route refresh persistence, sequential routing behavior, and source-PDF sync behavior needed for live hosted signing.
Completed a broad SDK/outreach pass: package build fixes, engine upload flow, consumer-app integration work, and public docs alignment with the current API/SDK surface.
Cleaned the public docs surface so internal runbooks/strategy are no longer consumer-visible, then added public-safe screenshots and code snippets across guides/API/blog pages.
Automated first-batch outreach prep and sending:
`npm run outreach:prep` generates the send sheet and queue.
`npm run outreach:send` sends source-backed rows through Resend and writes a ledger/report.
Copperlane and Veritus were sent successfully.
Mayflower, EigenPal, and Brickwise were skipped/held automatically instead of guessing unverified emails.
Re-ran the main build/test/smoke suite and recorded the latest exhaustive status in `docs-site/internal/operations/testing-status-2026-04-11-exhaustive.md`.
Fixed live signing-request request creation and invite delivery hardening:
`create-signing-request` no longer fails at the Supabase gateway with `401 Invalid JWT`
production SendGrid secrets are configured
invite delivery now succeeds again from the production edge flow
provider fallback logic exists so missing SendGrid config does not silently kill invites in the future
inProgress: The repo is now being maintained from `master` after a long integration/fix phase; launch-readiness work is mostly incremental rather than foundational.
Outreach can continue internally through `outreach-app`, but it should not be deployed publicly unless protected by Cloudflare Access or equivalent internal auth.
Public docs are now consumer-safe, but they still need periodic judgment calls so internal/ops material does not drift back into the public surface.

Aadhaar eSign exists as staged product groundwork, but the customer-safe state is "present in code, off in production by default" until Digio credentials, webhook validation, and operator readiness are all deliberately confirmed.
India-focused flows are intentionally reduced rather than expanded for the moment; stability and truth-in-surface take priority over unfinished route exposure.
Final ops/outreach follow-through is still ongoing: response tracking, reply/objection handling, email/domain sanity checks, and any last-mile regressions found during manual usage.
Mobile validation is materially better than the original smoke app, but it is still a validation shell rather than a production mobile product.
Session-expiry handling in the sender UI still needs a final UI-layer decision:
the shared signing-request service now returns a typed `session_expired` error after retry
redirect/login recovery should be handled by pages/auth boundaries, not inside the shared service itself
nextUp: If deploying the public app, build/deploy only `pdf-esigner`; do not deploy `outreach-app` to the public site.
Keep `VITE_AADHAAR_ESIGN_ENABLED=false` in production until Digio webhook delivery, signed-PDF completion flow, and operator runbook steps are validated end-to-end in a controlled environment.
Do not re-expose `India Desk` publicly until its product scope is intentionally revived and tested.
Decide whether generated outreach reports should remain committed as private audit artifacts or be moved to ignored storage.
Track replies and objections from the first automated outreach sends in `outreach/FIRST_BATCH_TRACKER.md`.
Add source-backed emails for Mayflower, EigenPal, and Brickwise if found; the sender will pick them up on a future run.
Keep the outreach claim discipline: lead with PDF tools plus embedded/hosted signing, and do not claim provider-backed identity verification as live.
Keep the launch/outreach checklist current as remaining manual/operator items close.
Use the Flutter smoke app as a validation target for SDK/mobile changes, but do not treat it as a launch-ready customer app yet.
Continue tightening polish issues found during real usage, especially around send/email edge cases, signer UX, and consumer integrations.
Add a deliberate UI response for `session_expired` in sender-facing pages/panels instead of relying on service-layer redirects.
Preserve the public/internal docs split and keep handoff notes current whenever release-facing behavior changes.
Finish any remaining collateral/workflow updates needed for outbound outreach and broader launch confidence.
Finish MCP launch operations outside the repo:
deploy the hosted Worker with production secrets
verify Smithery / MCP Registry listings use the current public repo identity
add the connect badge on the marketing site
Use Search Console to request indexing for `/`, `/pricing`, `/developers`, `/docs`, `/blog`, and the keyword pages now that the updated sitemap/routes are live
blockers: The repo lives inside a shared git root, so unrelated sibling changes can still confuse task ownership and status interpretation.
`.env` exists locally, so setup-sensitive work must avoid exposing secrets or license details.
Aadhaar rollout is configuration-sensitive: enabling the flag without valid `DIGIO_CLIENT_ID`, `DIGIO_CLIENT_SECRET`, and `DIGIO_WEBHOOK_SECRET` will correctly fail closed.
Some final launch items remain operational rather than code-only: provider account readiness, production email health, support/contact plumbing, and human validation steps.
Public docs now intentionally exclude internal ops/strategy; future edits must maintain that separation.

narrative.currentGoal: Push the hardened release batch, keep the public app/docs truthful about what is live, and deploy only the stable customer-facing surface while leaving incomplete Aadhaar/India-specific flows disabled by default.
narrative.inProgress: The repo is now being maintained from `master` after a long integration/fix phase; launch-readiness work is mostly incremental rather than foundational.

projects.name: pawPetrol
projects.progressLabel: both pipelines are documented; detective_dog_ai now has hardened proof runs across all supported characters plus manifests and quality gating
currentGoal: Make `detective_dog_ai` reproducible enough to become the explicit primary pipeline, while keeping `detective-biscuit-factory` as a documented reference until that decision is formalized.
doneRecently: Confirmed the repo is really two separate nested Python projects with different maturity levels:
`detective-biscuit-factory`: a linear prototype pipeline with story, planning, images, narration, subtitles, audio mix, and export stages
`detective_dog_ai`: a richer pipeline with character rules, validation and retry loops, scene graphing, asset generation/caching, narration, timing, overlays, subtitles, and export
Verified `detective-biscuit-factory` has one complete checked-in artifact set, but the core generators still write mock story, image, audio, and video data.
Verified `detective_dog_ai` has many saved episode JSON files and exported reels, which is the strongest evidence that this is the project that has actually been iterated locally.
Added root and subproject `README.md` files so future agents have an execution-oriented handoff path instead of relying only on source inspection.
Added `detective_dog_ai/.env.example` to document the current environment surface.
Fixed the most obvious `detective_dog_ai` handoff traps:
character metadata now follows the selected character in the scene graph path
character profile loading is more tolerant of mixed YAML schemas
the pipeline no longer mixes background music twice at final export
Ran and documented a full `detective_dog_ai` validation pass for `detective_cat`, producing:
`detective_dog_ai/data/episodes/episode_024.json`
`detective_dog_ai/data/reels/episode_014.mp4`
Updated subtitle handling in `detective_dog_ai` so missing FFmpeg subtitle-filter support is detected once and logged clearly.
Ran a real-key `detective_dog_ai` pass (OpenAI story + TTS active), producing:
`detective_dog_ai/data/episodes/episode_030.json`
`detective_dog_ai/data/reels/episode_015.mp4`
Added retention fallbacks for validator stability when the model misses strict question/catchphrase requirements.
Completed a subtitle-burn proof run using subtitle-capable FFmpeg, producing:
`detective_dog_ai/data/episodes/episode_032.json`
`detective_dog_ai/data/reels/episode_016.mp4`
burned-subtitle scene clips in `detective_dog_ai/data/clips/subbed_scene_*.mp4`
Added configurable narration quality modes to `detective_dog_ai` and validated `natural` mode with:
`detective_dog_ai/data/episodes/episode_033.json`
`detective_dog_ai/data/reels/episode_017.mp4`
Added completion-time usage reporting in the pipeline; direct credit-balance lookup remains limited by OpenAI secret-key billing API restrictions.
Added quality-gated story retries to `detective_dog_ai`, so weak-but-valid stories can be retried before the expensive media stages.
Added run manifests and explicit asset-fallback telemetry to `detective_dog_ai`, making it clearer when a successful run still depended on mock visual generation.
Completed a real-key + subtitle-burn + `natural` narration proof run for `detective_bunny`, producing:
`detective_dog_ai/data/episodes/episode_036.json`
`detective_dog_ai/data/reels/episode_019.mp4`
`detective_dog_ai/data/manifests/20260403T081630Z_episode_036_success.json`
Fixed `detective_dog_ai` batch-export cleanup so source clips are explicitly released between episodes.
Completed hardened proof runs for the remaining supported `detective_dog_ai` characters:
`detective_biscuit`
`detective_dog_ai/data/episodes/episode_039.json`
`detective_dog_ai/data/reels/episode_020.mp4`
`detective_dog_ai/data/manifests/20260403T082036Z_episode_039_success.json`
`detective_cat`
`detective_dog_ai/data/episodes/episode_040.json`
`detective_dog_ai/data/reels/episode_021.mp4`
`detective_dog_ai/data/manifests/20260403T082454Z_episode_040_success.json`
inProgress: The repo direction is still drifting from the simpler Biscuit prototype toward `detective_dog_ai`, but the handoff docs do not yet state that as a final canonical decision.
`detective_dog_ai` has substantial uncommitted work around characters, narration, validation, subtitles, scheduling, Docker, and scripts.
`detective_dog_ai` now has hardened proof coverage for all supported characters, but its visual generation remains partly mock-backed.
nextUp: Add a real music asset and prove end-to-end background music mixing in `detective_dog_ai`.
Finish the remaining `detective_dog_ai` cleanup around unused/stale paths and validate all supported characters with the same hardened runbook.
Decide whether `detective-biscuit-factory` remains maintained or should be explicitly labeled as superseded/reference-only.
Replace or explicitly de-scope the mock visual-generation path in `detective_dog_ai` if production imagery is required.
blockers: The repo contains overlapping pipelines, which increases drift and makes it unclear which code path is authoritative.
`detective_dog_ai` still contains mock fallbacks for visual generation stages; manifests now surface that more clearly, but the dependency is still a product risk.
`detective-biscuit-factory` outputs are placeholder artifacts rather than proof of real media generation.
Local model, asset, FFmpeg, and API-key dependencies are implied by the code but still are not documented at the root.
Because the root folder is not its own git repo, status can be missed unless agents also inspect the nested repos directly.

narrative.currentGoal: Make `detective_dog_ai` reproducible enough to become the explicit primary pipeline, while keeping `detective-biscuit-factory` as a documented reference until that decision is formalized.
narrative.inProgress: The repo direction is still drifting from the simpler Biscuit prototype toward `detective_dog_ai`, but the handoff docs do not yet state that as a final canonical decision.

currentGoal: Mature the current proof of concept into a documented, testable product for evaluating challenge or verification-system resilience.
doneRecently: Built a React + Vite frontend with a landing page, input flow, result cards, and recommendation display.
Built a Node/Express backend with:
health endpoint
capture flow
AI-analysis call
score calculation
Wired the frontend to call the backend and display the returned analysis, risk level, and captured image.
Added Dockerfiles plus a root `docker-compose.yml`.
Added a root README documenting the product shape, local run path, and current MVP limitations.
inProgress: The app is beyond a blank scaffold, but several pieces still read like a prototype:
frontend README is still the default Vite template
backend has no real test script
the operational setup is not documented from the root
nextUp: Replace the frontend template README with product-specific docs and add a root README.
Add one reproducible local run path that covers frontend, backend, and any model dependencies.
Add tests around score calculation and API-level validation, then record known limitations clearly.
blockers: The root project has no README, which makes handoff quality poor.
